ucs.title: [LLM] Bluenoroff (Sapphire Sleet / TA444) ISO/IMG/VHD container drop followed by rundll32/regsvr32 DLL execution from mounted volume
ucs.description: Catches Bluenoroff's signature 'Hidden Risk' / RustBucket-Windows / fake-Calendly delivery chain in which a recruiter or 'investor' lure email drops an ISO, IMG, VHD or LNK-in-ZIP container (used to defeat Mark-of-the-Web), the user mounts it, and a Bluenoroff-staged DLL is loaded via rundll32/regsvr32/msiexec from inside the container path within minutes — a chain documented across SnatchCrypto, AppleJeus and the 2024 Hidden Risk crypto-targeting campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as container_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhd" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhdx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\INetCache\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | rename Filesystem.* as * | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as exec_time values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="msiexec.exe") AND (Processes.process="*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*.cpl*" OR Processes.process="*.msi*" OR Processes.process="*,#1*" OR Processes.process="*,DllRegisterServer*") AND NOT (Processes.parent_process_path="C:\\Windows\\*") by Processes.dest | rename Processes.* as * ] | where exec_time>=container_time AND exec_time<=container_time+1800 | eval delay_sec=exec_time-container_time | table container_time exec_time delay_sec dest user file_name file_path parent cmdline | sort 0 - container_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let Containers = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img"
         or FileName endswith ".vhd" or FileName endswith ".vhdx"
         or FileName endswith ".lnk"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\",
                                @"\INetCache\", @"\Outlook\")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe",
                                            "firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe",
                                            "7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
    | project ContainerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              ContainerFile = FileName, ContainerPath = FolderPath;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","msiexec.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll", ",#1", "DllRegisterServer", ".cpl", ".msi", ".ocx")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[A-Z]:\\Users\\[^\\]+\\(Downloads|AppData\\Local\\Temp)\\"
| join kind=inner Containers on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + WindowMin)
| project ContainerTime, ExecTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ContainerTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, ContainerFile, ContainerPath,
          LolBin = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by ContainerTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let Containers = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType =~ "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".iso" or TargetFileName endswith ".img"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".vhd" or TargetFileName endswith ".vhdx"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".lnk"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\",
                                    @"\INetCache\", @"\Outlook\")
    | where ActingProcessName has_any ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe",
                                        "firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe",
                                        "7zg.exe","winrar.exe")
    | project ContainerTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              ActorUsername, ContainerFile = TargetFileName,
              ContainerPath = TargetFilePath;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\msiexec.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (".dll", ",#1", "DllRegisterServer", ".cpl", ".msi", ".ocx")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[A-Z]:\\Users\\[^\\]+\\(Downloads|AppData\\Local\\Temp)\\"
     or CommandLine has @"\Volumes\"
| extend HostKey = tolower(Computer)
| join kind=inner (Containers | extend HostKey = tolower(DvcHostname)) on HostKey
| where TimeGenerated between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + WindowMin)
| project ContainerTime, ExecTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ContainerTime),
          Computer, Account, ContainerFile, ContainerPath,
          LolBin = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine,
          ParentImage = ParentProcessName
| order by ContainerTime desc
ucs.rationale: Bluenoroff/TA444/APT38 has used ISO+LNK and IMG/VHD container delivery as a Mark-of-the-Web evasion since at least 2022 and rotated through it heavily in the 2023 RustBucket / 2024 Hidden Risk crypto-firm campaigns documented by Kaspersky GReAT, Microsoft (Sapphire Sleet), Mandiant and SentinelOne. Their staged DLL is consistently loaded by rundll32/regsvr32/msiexec from inside the mounted contain

ucs.title: [LLM] Bluenoroff SnatchCrypto / KandyKorn Chromium credential & session-cookie exfiltration (non-browser process accessing Login Data / Cookies /
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation step Bluenoroff uses across SnatchCrypto, AppleJeus and KandyKorn: a stage-2 implant (PowerShell, rundll32-loaded DLL, signed-but-unsigned-by-MS binary, or python.exe) reads Chromium-family Login Data, Cookies, Web Data and Local State files to harvest cryptocurrency-exchange session cookies and stored passwords, often paired with clipboard hijacking to swap wallet addresses — the financial-theft signature behaviour that distinguishes this DPRK financially-motivated cluster from espionage actors.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="Login Data" OR Filesystem.file_name="Cookies" OR Filesystem.file_name="Local State" OR Filesystem.file_name="Web Data" OR Filesystem.file_name="Login Data For Account") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\BraveSoftware\\Brave-Browser\\User Data\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path Filesystem.process_guid | rename Filesystem.* as * | search NOT process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","OneDrive.exe","explorer.exe") | join type=left process_guid [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_guid | rename Processes.* as * ] | where count>=2 OR process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","python.exe","node.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","curl.exe") | table first_seen last_seen dest user process_name process_path parent_process cmdline files paths count | sort 0 - last_seen
ucs.kql:
let Lookback = 14d;
let BrowserOwnedProcs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe",
                                "opera.exe","vivaldi.exe","googleupdate.exe",
                                "microsoftedgeupdate.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe",
                                "onedrive.exe","explorer.exe","searchprotocolhost.exe",
                                "mssense.exe","msmpeng.exe"]);
let HighRiskLolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe",
                               "mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","python.exe",
                               "pythonw.exe","node.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe",
                               "bitsadmin.exe","esentutl.exe"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Login Data For Account","Cookies","Local State","Web Data")
     or FileName endswith ".sqlite" and FolderPath has @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
                            @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
                            @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\",
                            @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\")
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) !in (BrowserOwnedProcs)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| extend IsLolBin = tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (HighRiskLolBins)
| extend IsUnsignedTemp = InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any
     (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Roaming\")
| where IsLolBin or IsUnsignedTemp
     or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName == ""
     or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"(?i)\\Volumes?\\|\\Mount\\"
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            FilesTouched = make_set(FileName, 16),
            Paths = make_set(FolderPath, 8),
            FileCount = count()
            by DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
               ProcImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
               ProcPath  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
               ProcCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
               ProcSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
               ProcCompany = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName
| where FileCount >= 2 or array_length(FilesTouched) >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Lookback = 14d;
let BrowserOwnedProcs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe",
                                "opera.exe","vivaldi.exe","googleupdate.exe",
                                "microsoftedgeupdate.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe",
                                "onedrive.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let HighRiskLolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe",
                               "mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","python.exe",
                               "pythonw.exe","node.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe",
                               "bitsadmin.exe","esentutl.exe"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventType in~ ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where TargetFileName in~ ("Login Data","Login Data For Account","Cookies","Local State","Web Data")
| where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
                                @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
                                @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\",
                                @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\")
| extend ProcLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ProcLeaf !in (BrowserOwnedProcs)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend IsLolBin = ProcLeaf in (HighRiskLolBins)
| extend IsUnsignedTemp = ActingProcessName has_any
     (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Roaming\")
| where IsLolBin or IsUnsignedTemp
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            FilesTouched = make_set(TargetFileName, 16),
            Paths = make_set(TargetFilePath, 8),
            FileCount = count()
            by DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
               ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
| where FileCount >= 2 or array_length(FilesTouched) >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Bluenoroff Chromium Credential and Cookie File Access by Non-Browser Process
id: 8d3f2bc4-7a11-4b23-9b1d-bluenoroff-cookie-theft
status: experimental
description: Detects non-browser processes (PowerShell, rundll32, python, mshta, unsigned temp binaries) opening Chromium Login Data / Cookies / Local State / Web Data files — Bluenoroff/TA444 SnatchCrypto, AppleJeus and KandyKorn cryptocurrency credential theft signature.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/the-bluenoroff-cryptocurrency-hunt-is-still-on/105488/
  - https://www.kaspersky.com/about/press-releases/bluenoroff-targets-crypto-startups-snatchcrypto
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/12/sapphire-sleet
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0098/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1539
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.g0098
logsource:
  category: file_access
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_browser_files:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\'
      - '\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\'
      - '\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\Login Data'
      - '\Login Data For Account'
      - '\Cookies'
      - '\Local State'
      - '\Web Data'
  filter_legit_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\opera.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection_browser_files and not filter_legit_browsers
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - TargetFilename
falsepositives:
  - Endpoint backup or DLP agents that index user profile data — allowlist by signed publisher
  - Browser-data-migration tools run during user provisioning
level: high
ucs.rationale: Bluenoroff is the financially-motivated DPRK cluster (CrowdStrike Stardust Chollima, MS Sapphire Sleet, Mandiant APT38, US Treasury BeagleBoyz) whose entire raison d'être is monetising compromise of cryptocurrency exchanges, DeFi platforms and SWIFT-connected banks — making browser-stored crypto-exchange session cookies and saved exchange logins their prize artefact. Across SnatchCrypto (Kaspersky
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
